---
title: "UIAO Programs"
subtitle: "Training, Test Plans, Project Plans, Education"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "The four canonical programs that govern how the UIAO substrate is learned, tested, planned, and onboarded."
aspirational: true
---

The UIAO substrate ships with four canonical programs. Each has a
canon spec (the authority, in `src/uiao/canon/specs/`), a narrative
walkthrough (the explainer, in `docs/narrative/`), and a page on
this site linking both together.

| Program | Canon spec | Narrative walkthrough | Audience |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Training** | [UIAO_125](uiao_125.qmd) | [One program, two tracks](../narrative/program-training.qmd) | Internal contributors + external operators |
| **Test Plans** | [UIAO_126](uiao_126.qmd) | [The catalog, not the plans](../narrative/program-test-plans.qmd) | Internal + external |
| **Project Plans** | [UIAO_127](uiao_127.qmd) | [Roadmap & templates](../narrative/program-project-plans.qmd) | Internal roadmap + external agency templates |
| **Education** | [UIAO_128](uiao_128.qmd) | [The comics tell the story](../narrative/program-education.qmd) | Primarily external (agency onboarding) |

## How they relate

**Education** is where you start if you've never seen UIAO —
narrative-first, comic-led, builds the mental model.

**Training** is where you go once the mental model is in place —
pick Contributor or Operator track, follow the curriculum, land
your first PR or your first clean `uiao substrate walk`.

**Test Plans** is the catalog auditors and contributors consult to
know which tests govern which behavior. Every test has a canonical
entry here — no plans live only in someone's head.

**Project Plans** is the pair of the internal roadmap (what the
substrate will do next, ADR-anchored) and the agency templates
(deterministic paths from empty clone to first OSCAL artifact).

## Governance

All four program specs are CANONICAL (`classification: CANONICAL` in
their frontmatter). They are governed by the canon-change protocol
in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md):
a substantive change lands with an ADR, a schema check, and a drift
scan. The narrative walkthroughs are derived consumers — they cite
the canon spec they derive from, not the other way around.
